message:
Improve overscan handling and dual-mode rendering in sequence grid

VirtualScroller.ts:
- Refactor into computeRange(overscanRows) private method
- getVisibleRange() returns viewport-only range (for UI logic)
- getRenderRange() returns overscan-expanded range (for rendering)
- Fix offsetY/offsetX calculation to be relative to startRow/startCol
  rather than using modulo, which broke when overscan shifted the start

WebGLSequenceRenderer.ts:
- Remove ANGLE_instanced_arrays extension check - WebGL2 has native
  instanced rendering, no extension needed
- Add getRowHeight() helper for dual view mode (2x cell height)
- Update scroller itemHeight when viewMode changes between dna/dual
- Use getRenderRange() for rendering to include overscan
- Use scroll state directly for u_scrollOffset uniform instead of
  range offsets, which were incorrect with overscan

CanvasSequenceGridRenderer.ts:
- Minor adjustments to align with VirtualScroller API changes

useSequenceGrid.ts:
- Wire through renderer option changes

shaders.ts:
- Shader constant adjustments for offset handling

These changes fix visual glitches when scrolling with overscan enabled,
particularly in dual view mode where row heights double.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
